warnings: logP from semi-empirical ΔG_solv differences is screening-grade; ±1 log unit typical.

Each leg is an electronic-energy difference on a single frozen geometry — no ZPE/thermal/entropy and no per-phase relaxation. The reported delta_G_solv_* legs are electronic only; the 1 atm→1 M standard-state term cancels in the water–octanol difference, so logP itself is insensitive to it, but the individual legs are not corrected ΔG*_solv.
For chemoinformatic logP, also consider RDKit's Crippen/XLogP (group-contribution).

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: logP (log10 partition coefficient, n-octanol/water, dimensionless).
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding; no Gaussian basis), as implemented in xtb.
Geometry provenance: single-point electronic energies evaluated on the user-supplied ibuprofen geometry (/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/logp-partition-validation/ibuprofen/ibuprofen.xyz); no geometry optimization was performed (single-conformer assumption, no Boltzmann averaging).
Solvent model: ALPB implicit solvation in xtb for both water and n-octanol; the logP is obtained from the electronic ΔG_solv difference between these two continua. No explicit solvent molecules.
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral singlet).
Software: xtb via chemkit logp skill (backend version not reported).
Convergence & caveats: each semi-empirical single-point SCF converged (integrity status OK). All warnings from the engine are reproduced above verbatim.

Live output & reproducibility: the run’s stdout/stderr streamed to a logp_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory while it executed; see that file for the full SCF traces and command header. CLI invocation recorded in the result JSON: `/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py logp --method xtb …`.

Headline result (GFN2-xTB, gas phase ΔG_solv differences): logP = 3.6631299.
